purpose: Documentation page for the configuration area: Theming. It explains operational behavior, user workflow, data ownership, or architecture details that future agents use when updating Synapse docs or app behavior. Key content: Synapse uses a dark-first design system built on CSS custom properties, Google Fonts, and a consistent status color palette shared between CSS and JavaScript. The visual language is characterized by dark backgrounds, translucent surfaces with backdrop blur (glass morphism), purple-to-green gradient accents, and color-coded status indicators. The file is 542 lines long and should be treated as a knowledge/control-plane source rather than application code.
gotchas: Documentation may describe behavior that has moved into Electron IPC/services or .claude skills; verify implementation before making broad user-facing claims.
patterns: Documentation pattern: H1 title, short introduction, then operational sections with headings, tables, examples, or path references for future maintainers.
Uses fenced code blocks for command examples, schemas, or prompt templates.
Uses markdown tables for compact command, file, or option matrices.
conventions: Markdown headings use a single H1 followed by H2/H3 sections for navigable documentation.
ASCII double hyphen is used in prose where many docs avoid Unicode punctuation for terminal readability.
